Title: Short-Term Test for Toxicogenomic Analysis of Ecotoxic Modes of Action in Lemna minor

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: lipids; sterols; isoprene precursors; carotenoids/ABA-related metabolites
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane integrity; growth; photosynthetic/light stress response; hormone-related signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate biomarker whose expression is differentially affected by atorvastatin and bentazon in short-term Lemna minor assay
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression profiling in 3-day exposure assay; proteomics context; comparative expression signature analysis under atorvastatin and bentazon
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lminor_013527 was highlighted as a promising biomarker candidate for discriminating compound mode of action. 2. Its expression differed between atorvastatin and bentazon treatments. 3. Specific molecular function for nutrient metabolism was not experimentally validated in this paper.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed after 72 h exposure to atorvastatin and/or bentazon at EC5 and EC20
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: ArrayExpress E-MTAB-11459 (atorvastatin), E-MTAB-11460 (bentazon); proteomics: PRIDE PXD031680 (atorvastatin), PXD031679 (bentazon)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to atorvastatin (HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor) or bentazon (PSII inhibitor) for 3 days under continuous light
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential RT-qPCR biomarker for screening ecotoxic mode of action in Lemna minor
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper does not establish this gene as a causal metabolism gene; it is only proposed as a biomarker candidate.
